Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Second Deposition Of A. Joseph Salais Ph.D.
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Edward Sears, Esq. 415-438-4600 of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin & Hansen LLP for Defendant/Cross-Complainant CROSSLINK, INC. DBA WESTAR MARINE SERVICES
John Faulconer, Esq. 703-862-5550 of Lewis Brisbois for Cross-Defendant Dr. Joseph Salais.

RULING - A R G U E D. Atty. Sears argues and requests for additional minutes. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
marc A. Centor, Esq, of Cox wootton Lerner, et al, 415-438-4600 appearing for Cross complaintant westar Marine Service, et al;
John W. Faulconer Esq., Lewis Brisbois, Telephone 415-4385901, appeaerinf for Crossdefendnat De. Salais;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT A. JOSEPH SALAIS, PH.D.'S DEMURRER TO the cross-complaint filed by defendant and cross-complainant Crosslink, Inc. dba Westar Marine Services

OVERRULED. Westar has sufficiently pled facts supporting its claims for indemnity and contribution based on the alleged fault of Dr. Salais in causing plaintiff Grissom's injuries. Although the pleadings do not allege what specific acts by Dr. Salais constitute a breach of the applicable standard of care, general allegations in a medical malpractice cause of action are sufficient to withstand a general demurrer. (See Landeros v. Flood (1976) 17 Cal.3d 399, 407 ["'In this state negligence may be pleaded in general terms, and that is as true of malpractice cases as it is of other types of negligence cases.'"].) "Negligence may be alleged in general terms; that is, it is sufficient to allege an act was negligently done without stating the particular omission which rendered it negligent." (Hahn v. Mirda (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 740, 746-747 [allegations that defendant doctors "negligently cared for, diagnosed and treated" plaintiff were sufficient].) The cross-complaint sufficiently alleges that Dr. Salais, a psychologist, undertook the diagnosis, care and treatment of plaintiff on or about May 18, 2017 and that he failed to exercise proper degree of care and skills and performed unnecessary and negligent treatment resulting in personal injuries (Cross-Complaint 2, 11, 12). The cross-complaint is not subject to a special demurrer because it adequately puts Dr. Salais on notice of the claims against him to enable him to file an answer. "A demurrer for uncertainty is strictly construed, even where a complaint is in some respects uncertain, because ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures," especially when the particular facts are within the presumptive knowledge of the defendant, as in the case at hand. (Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.)

Order signed,

Judge: Ethan P. Sculman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn CSR6185, Tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Crosslink, Inc. Dba Westar Marine Services Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
marc A. Centor, Esq, of Cox wootton Lerner, et al, 415-438-4600 appearing for Cross complaintant westar Marine Service, et al;
John W. Faulconer Esq., Lewis Brisbois, Telephone 415-4385901, appeaerinf for Crossdefendnat De. Salais;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT A. JOSEPH SALAIS, PH.D.'s Motion To Strike Crosslink, Inc. Dba Westar Marine Services Cross-Complaint

DENIED and the motion to strike paragraphs praying for attorney fees and associated costs is GRANTED with 20 days leave to amend. The Court treats Westar's opposition to Dr. Salais's motion to strike as the equivalent of a motion for leave to file a cross-complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c) and grants leave per the stipulation between Westar and plaintiff Robert Grissom. The cross-complaint does not allege any statutory or contractual basis for its prayer for attorney fees and costs, nor does it allege facts supporting the application of the tort of another doctrine. The tort of another doctrine applies when one "through the tort of another has been required to act in the protection of his interests by bringing or defending an action against a third person." (Lederer v. Gursey Schneider LLP (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 508, 527.) The rule has been strictly applied and "nearly all of the cases which have applied the [Prentice v. North Amer. Title Guar. Corp. (1963) 59 Cal.2d 618] doctrine involve a clear violation of a traditional tort duty between the tortfeasor who is required to pay the attorney fees and the person seeking compensation for those fees. . . The rule of Prentice was not intended to apply to one of several joint tortfeasors in order to justify additional attorney fee damages." (David v. Hermann (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 672, 688-689.) The cross-complaint does not allege any duty owed by the alleged indemnitor to the indemnitee. Furthermore, the cross-complaint does not allege that Westar had to defend a lawsuit because of Dr. Salais' tortious conduct; rather, Westar has to defend because plaintiff sustained an industrial injury. (See UMET Trust v. Santa Monica Medical Investment Co. (1983) 140 Cal.App.3d 864, 866 ["The exceptional circumstances doctrine is not applied in cases where the party seeking reimbursement for counsel fees is not compelled or required to bring or defend an action against the third person."]). Westar is given leave to amend if it can allege in good faith facts supporting a prayer for attorney's fees and associated costs.

Order is signend

Judge: Ethan P. Sculman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn CSR6185, Tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)
